danger_signals.visual_form: Hook's pirate henchmen sprites, spiders, snakes, skeletons
danger_signals.meaning: Combat threat requiring evasion or engagement

danger_signals.visual_form: Leaf-based health meter (max 4 leaves, player starts with 2)
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative damage taken; losing all leaves results in level restart

danger_signals.visual_form: Golden sword drops from Peter Pan when hit; reverts to dagger
danger_signals.meaning: Status downgrade after damage; offensive capability reduced until sword recovered

danger_signals.visual_form: Caves, forests, lagoons, snowy mountains with navigational challenges

safety_signals.meaning: Refill health meter; consumable healing resource

safety_signals.visual_form: Pixie dust pickups; Tinker Bell visual presence
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invulnerability to platforming hazards; movement advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Tinker Bell sprite interactions throughout levels
safety_signals.meaning: Extension of flight duration; safety window for aerial traversal

safety_signals.visual_form: Golden sword acquisition after level 1 completion
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive upgrade enabling ranged energy attacks; status improvement

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Neverland locations (caves, forests, lagoons, snowy mountains)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pirate henchmen as visual enemy type
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Pirate Town setting; Hook's ship presence

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Distinct sprite designs per creature type
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Natural Neverland hazards throughout level environments
Present throughout game levels for mechanical support

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dagger as primary weapon; basic enemy encounters; foundational platforming
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Limited offensive range; reliance on melee combat and evasion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Golden sword equipped; increased enemy density and complexity; varied terrain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ranged energy attacks available; greater offensive versatility; flight mechanics introduced

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All enemy types present; complex level layouts across diverse biomes; visual intensification
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Maximum challenge state; flight meter management critical; preparation for Hook confrontation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Captain Hook as boss opponent; sword duel setting; climactic visual framing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss battle mechanics; one-on-one combat emphasis; narrative conclusion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Leaf meter visually depleted; Peter Pan sprite may show damage states
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: High vulnerability state; one hit from failure; enemy avoidance becomes priority

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Peter Pan sprite in flight animation; Fly Meter visible and consuming
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Temporary invulnerability to ground hazards; elevated movement capabilities

environmental_storytelling.example: Progression through caves → forests → lagoons → snowy mountains visually tracks journey deeper into Neverland and toward Hook's territory

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy type density as difficulty escalation
environmental_storytelling.example: Early levels feature sparse pirate henchmen; mid-levels introduce spiders and snakes; late levels combine all enemy types, signaling approach to final conflict

environmental_storytelling.technique: Weapon upgrade as power fantasy and progression
environmental_storytelling.example: Dagger-to-golden-sword transition after level 1 victory establishes Peter Pan's growing power and readiness to face Hook

environmental_storytelling.technique: Tinker Bell's presence as protective marker
environmental_storytelling.example: Tinker Bell sprite visibility in levels indicates availability of healing support and flight meter restoration—zones of relative safety within danger

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Normal movement speed; can engage enemies; full offensive capability with equipped weapon
Altered sprite appearance; restricted engagement options; prioritizes evasion over combat
Elevated sprite position; altered movement trajectory; temporary hazard invulnerability; Fly Meter countdown visible
Reverts to dagger; reduced attack range; must retrieve golden sword in subsequent level
Predictable movement patterns; aggro on player proximity; standard sprite animations
Environmental threats with distinct movement patterns; hazards rather than conscious enemies; engage automatically on contact
Provides visual cue for item pickups; appears at intervals to refill Fly Meter; ally indicator

notes: Hook (SNES) uses a leaf-based health system and pixie dust flight mechanics to communicate vulnerability and temporary power states. Weapon downgrades (sword loss) create visible status regression. Enemy variety (pirates vs. wildlife) signals both faction threat and environmental hazard types. Biome progression through Neverland serves dual narrative and difficulty scaling functions. Tinker Bell's presence is the game's primary visual safety signal. The game lacks explicit faction dialogue or political complexity—danger/safety communication relies on enemy sprite design, environmental layout, and mechanical feedback systems rather than lore or world-state narrative.
